decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain card advantage. The deck aims to control the board through a combination of removal spells like Snuff Out and Agony Warp, alongside card draw spells like Thought Scour and Mental Note. The inclusion of creatures like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic provide win conditions while the rest of the deck focuses on controlling the game and out-valuing the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counter spells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck focuses on tempo and resource management, using cards like Augur of Bolas and Faerie Seer to maintain board presence while controlling the opponent's threats with cards like Snuff Out and Cast Down. The deck aims to out-value the opponent by efficiently using its resources and gaining incremental advantages over time until it can secure a win condition with cards like Ninja of the Deep Hours or Gurmag Angler.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent by using burn spells and creatures with abilities that trigger whenever a spell is cast. The deck includes efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, along with creatures like Guttersnipe and Kessig Flamebreather to continually pressure the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting to help dig for key cards and Sneaky Snacker to provide additional damage. Overall, the deck aims to close out the game as quickly as possible through direct damage.

This Pauper Aggro-Burn deck focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently to the opponent using a combination of direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, alongside creature-based burn like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of damage while also gaining card advantage through spells like Grab the Prize and Faithless Looting. The deck is designed to keep the pressure on the opponent from the early turns of the game, leading to a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to establish board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Snuff Out, and Agony Warp, the deck aims to keep the opponent's threats at bay while digging for answers and maintaining a presence on the board through creatures like Spellstutter Sprite and Augur of Bolas. The deck also includes cards like Brainstorm and Ninja of the Deep Hours to provide additional card advantage and flexibility in play. By managing resources efficiently, the deck seeks to outlast and outplay the opponent in a war of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly flood the board with Elf creatures and generate mana with cards like Priest of Titania and Llanowar Elves. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way provide card advantage by allowing you to dig deeper into your deck. Masked Vandal and Generous Ent offer flexibility by serving as removal or boosting your own creatures. Timberwatch Elf can quickly pump up your other Elves for massive damage, making this deck a fast and aggressive threat in the Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

This Gruul Aggro deck focuses on ramping up quickly with Utopia Sprawl and Wild Growth to play big creatures like Avenging Hunter and Annoyed Altisaur. It also utilizes Eldrazi Repurposer and Jewel Thief to disrupt opponents' strategies while maintaining pressure on the board with creatures like Boarding Party and Sagu Wildling. Malevolent Rumble and Writhing Chrysalis provide additional removal options, making it difficult for opponents to stabilize. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells.

The key concept of this deck is to utilize the Urzatron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing you to cast powerful spells and creatures such as Maelstrom Colossus and Bramble Wurm. The deck also includes control elements such as Relic of Progenitus and Bojuka Bog to disrupt your opponent's graveyard strategies. Additionally, cards like Ancient Stirrings and Expedition Map help you find your Urzatron lands and other key pieces of your strategy, ensuring consistency in your gameplay.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Terror deck is to control the game through counterspells and card draw, while also utilizing creatures like Cryptic Serpent and Murmuring Mystic to apply pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain card advantage through efficient spells like Counterspell and Ponder. By controlling the board and out-valuing the opponent, the deck can eventually overwhelm them with powerful threats like Tolarian Terror. This deck excels in dictating the pace of the game and outmaneuvering the opponent with careful planning and resource management.

This deck is focused on using Gates to gain an advantage over the opponent. It uses control elements like removal spells and protection to outlast the opponent while generating value from the Gates. The key strategy is to stall the game with cards like Sacred Cat and Prismatic Strands, while building up resources with cards like Basilisk Gate and Citadel Gate. This allows the player to control the board and ultimately win the game through attrition and value generation.

This Mono Blue Aggro deck focuses on efficiently casting low-cost creatures and spells while disrupting opponents with counterspells and bounce effects. By utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Cryptic Serpent, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent while maintaining control through counter and card draw spells like Counterspell and Brainstorm. With a focus on tempo and card advantage, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents and disrupt their game plan, ultimately securing a victory through aggressive and strategic play.

The primary focus of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures such as Annoyed Altisaur and Avenging Hunter while ramping up mana with cards like Utopia Sprawl and Arbor Elf. Writhing Chrysalis provides additional threats, while spells like Mwonvuli Acid-Moss and Structural Distortion disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent, utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to maintain control of the game.

This Ephemerate control deck focuses on leveraging the flicker ability of Ephemerate to generate card advantage and control the game. By blinking key creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer, as well as reusing powerful ETB effects like Counterspell and Augur of Bolas, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast its opponent. With a mix of removal, card draw, and disruption, this deck looks to slow down the game and grind out incremental advantages until it can close out the game with a powerful threat or combo finish.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Aggro deck focuses on fast and efficient red creature and burn spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By using cards like Burning-Tree Emissary and Goblin Bushwhacker to flood the board early, combined with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to push through damage and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize. Additionally, cards like Rally at the Hornburg and Wrenn's Resolve provide ways to pump up creatures and give them additional reach, ensuring a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy and control the game through counterspells and removals. With cards like Counterspell, Snuff Out, and Agony Warp, the deck aims to prevent the opponent from developing their board while maintaining card advantage with Brainstorm and Lórien Revealed. The inclusion of Faerie creatures like Spellstutter Sprite and Faerie Seer provide additional disruption and card advantage. The win condition revolves around card advantage and incremental advantage through creature damage with cards like Thorn of the Black Rose and Ninja of the Deep Hours.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board through removal spells and card advantage, while generating value through sacrificing creatures and artifacts. The Golgari/Jund color combination offers a versatile range of removal options and graveyard hate, with some recursion potential. The deck aims to outlast the opponent with efficient removal spells and slowly build up advantage with card draw and token generation from Khalni Garden. The inclusion of cards like Snuff Out and Tithing Blade allows for flexibility in dealing with threats and maintaining board presence.
